---
title: "Course Study Guide"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: article
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: "margin=2.5cm"
canvas:
  type: study_guide
  published: true
  pdf:
    target_module: "Course Documents"
    filename: "KursPM.pdf"
    title: "Study Guide (Official)"
    published: true
---

## Welcome

This is the official study guide for the course. This section appears in
**both** the Canvas page and the regulatory PDF.

### Learning Outcomes

By the end of this course, students will be able to:

1. Write Python scripts for data processing
2. Use the Canvas API for automation
3. Apply best practices for code quality

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}

## Getting Started

Head over to the [first assignment](../02_Python Basics/02_Assignment.qmd) to begin.

### Useful Links

- [Python Documentation](https://docs.python.org)
- [Canvas API Reference](https://canvas.instructure.com/doc/api/)

> **Tip:** Use the module navigation on the left to browse all course material.

:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}

## Regulatory Information

This section is only included in the PDF version of the study guide.

| Field              | Value                          |
|--------------------|--------------------------------|
| Course code        | DT-101                         |
| Credits            | 7.5 ECTS                       |
| Level              | First cycle                    |
| Main field         | Computer Science               |
| Grading scale      | U / G / VG                     |
| Department         | Department of Computer Science |
| Examiner           | Dr. A. Example                 |

### Prerequisites

Completed introductory programming course (minimum 6 ECTS) or equivalent.

### Examination

- **Written exam** (4.5 ECTS) — individual, on-campus
- **Project assignment** (3.0 ECTS) — group work, oral presentation

### Course Literature

- Lutz, M. (2013). *Learning Python*. 5th ed. O'Reilly Media.
- Additional materials provided via Canvas.

:::
